patch.value: Every ecosystem audit manually mirrors open GitHub issues into pm items (GH-<n> title prefix, issue-code dedupe) and manually closes GH issues once fixes ship (see pm-7cx8, pm-hq0r). The importer/exporter mechanism (pm-7qjk, exemplars pm-13bn) makes this a natural package: bidirectional GitHub Issues sync built only on public capabilities. Long-term this generalizes to other trackers (Jira/Linear) as sibling packages — GitHub first because it dogfoods this repo's own loop. Depends on SDK-first promotion so the package composes primitives, not CLI internals.
pm-github package: GitHub Issues importer/exporter for bidirectional issue-item mirroring (automates the audit GH-coverage loop)

A pm-github package (registerImporter/registerExporter, package-first like pm-beads/pm-todos) imports GitHub issues as typed pm items with GH-<n> issue codes, dedupes against existing title-prefix convention, and exports/closes GH issues from closed pm items with evidence comments; dry-run mode; docs + linked tests; the manual per-audit GH-issue mirroring checklist becomes a single command

pm-github (github.com/unbraind/pm-github) is installed in this workspace and its import reconciles the ~26 manually mirrored GH-<n>-title-prefix items by adopting them (adds gh:owner/repo#N provenance tag) instead of duplicating; pm github sync can close a GitHub issue with an evidence comment citing the pm item's close_reason/resolution and maps pm closed->state_reason completed, canceled->not_planned (inverse of its import mapping); import seeds required metadata per the workspace metadata profile so pm validate stays warning-clean after import; all changes land as PRs on unbraind/pm-github with dry-run-first semantics preserved; one audit iteration runs the GH-coverage step via pm github import/sync instead of the manual checklist
Extend the existing pm-github package to close the audit loop: adopt GH-<n>-titled items into gh: provenance, evidence-comment close sync, metadata-profile seeding

## Context (corrected in audit #15 follow-up)
A mature pm-github package ALREADY EXISTS (github.com/unbraind/pm-github): idempotent import with gh:owner/repo#N provenance tags and incremental --since, export with dry-run-default upsert, pm github sync (close/reopen upstream), a github search provider, schema fields (github_url/number/state/author/...), preflight token checks, and validate diagnostics. Sibling sync packages also exist: pm-jira, pm-linear, pm-csv. This item was originally (and wrongly) filed as "create a pm-github package" — rewritten to EXTEND the existing one.

## Gaps vs the maintainer audit loop (this repo's workflow)
1. **Provenance adoption**: this tracker mirrors issues manually with a GH-<n> title prefix (issue-code convention, ~26 open mappings). pm-github's idempotency keys on gh:owner/repo#N tags, so a plain import would DUPLICATE every manually mirrored item. Extend import with a configurable pre-existing-item matcher (title-prefix regex / issue-code) that adopts matches by tagging them with provenance instead of creating new items.
2. **Evidence-comment closure**: the audit closes GH issues with a comment citing pm close_reason/resolution (pm-7cx8/pm-hq0r pattern). pm github sync closes/reopens but posts no evidence. Extend sync with an opt-in evidence comment and map pm closed->state_reason completed, canceled->not_planned (the inverse of its existing import mapping).
3. **Metadata-profile seeding**: imported items should seed required metadata (AC/estimate/author) per the workspace metadata profile so pm validate stays clean — same lesson as the Semgrep scanner imports (audit #13).

## Delivery
Extensions land as PRs on unbraind/pm-github preserving its dry-run-first semantics; then install the package in this workspace and run one audit iteration's GH-coverage step through pm github import/sync to retire the manual checklist. Generalization to other trackers is NOT this item — pm-jira/pm-linear already exist and would pick up the same patterns separately.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Correction (user feedback, audit #15 follow-up): pm-github already exists and is mature — this item is now scoped to extending it, not creating it. Ecosystem check performed: unbraind org already ships pm-jira, pm-linear, pm-csv (tracker sync), pm-ops (multi-repo fleet), pm-graph, pm-web, pm-brief, pm-context, and more. Rule going forward: before proposing any new package, enumerate gh repo list unbraind pm-* plus the bundled install-hint catalog.
